summary: Task #60: added qwen35moe GGUF expert pack8 selected prefill kernels and opt-in sidecar integration; correctness/profiler gates pass, but the transient sidecar path is slower than the current raw-GGUF fast-bulk default and remains a blocker for task #61.

blocker.summary: The correctness-safe sidecar path is opt-in and launches the packed kernels, but it copies/allocates/free sidecar arrays layer-by-layer because 24 GiB device budget cannot hold raw GGUF weights plus all packed sidecars. With default 128-thread parity-safe reductions it reaches 62.46 tok/s, which is +303.8% vs the old native row baseline but -37.5% vs the current raw selected fast-bulk default. Task #61 needs grouped/tiled reuse and/or a memory plan that avoids duplicate raw+sidecar residency.
next_actions: Profile layer-level selected kernels vs raw to separate copy overhead from compute overhead.
Design a grouped-by-expert/tiled kernel that reuses packed weights across rows selected for the same expert.
Evaluate sidecar residency policies that omit raw expert weights only if decode gets an equivalent fallback or sidecar decode path.
